lesson: A frozen block at a normal bowl temperature means the mix is under syrup ratio, not a refrigeration fault — sugar is the antifreeze. Drain and re-mix it.
symptoms: Left bowl frozen into one solid block.

tests.error-log: AUGER OVERTORQUE trips, repeating since this morning.
tests.temp-probe: Bowl temp right on setpoint. The refrigeration is doing exactly what it's told.
tests.inspect-beater: Bowl frozen into one solid mass around the auger. The auger itself is intact — stalling against the ice, not broken.

symptoms: Right bowl seized into a glacier overnight.
Drive motor breaker has tripped twice this morning.
Bowl frozen solid the day after the new hire's first solo refill.
Auger stalling with a groan you can hear from the counter.

flavour: Diagnosis: somebody was stretching the syrup.
authenticityNote: Sugar is the antifreeze in a slushie — mix that's under the syrup ratio freezes into a solid block instead of staying slush. A genuinely common operator error, usually someone economising on concentrate.
